summary: Map the vAmiga core API + web glue to plan an iOS app mirroring the AppleIIGS port

maps.API & LIFECYCLE:
# vAmiga Core — Lifecycle & Public API Engineering Map

## TL;DR thread model (read this first)

**This fork's core does NOT spawn its own thread.** In `/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/Infrastructure/Thread.cpp:17-27`, `Thread::launch()` calls only `initialize()` — the `thread = std::thread(&Thread::runLoop, this)` line is commented out (line 24), and `Thread::isLaunched()` is hardcoded `return true` (`Thread.h:86`). `isEmulatorThread()`/`isUserThread()` both hardcode `return true` (`Thread.h:101-102`). This is an Emscripten single-thread modification.

Consequence for the iOS bridge: **you own the emulation thread**. Spawn a background pthread in `EmulatorBridge`, and per host frame call `emu->amiga->emu->computeFrame()` (the `Emulator::computeFrame()` override, `Emulator.h:125`). This is exactly what the web build does in `wasm_execute()` (`main.cpp:392-398`) and `wasm_draw_one_frame()` (`main.cpp:402-579`). The built-in `runLoop()`/`execute()`/`sleep()` machinery (`Thread.cpp:38-137`) is dead code here. Do NOT rely on `wakeUp()` driving frames — in this fork nothing pumps `runLoop`.

All public types live in `namespace vamiga`. Headers use C++ `<filesystem>` (`fs::path`), exceptions (`AppError`, `Fault`), and `std::string` — the bridge `.mm` must be compiled as Objective-C++ with C++17/20.

---

## 1. Instantiation & sub-API objects

**Top class: `vamiga::VAmiga`** (`/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/VAmiga.h:1615`). It is the host facade; it internally owns a `vamiga::Emulator` (`Core/Infrastructure/Emulator.h:21`) which owns two `Amiga` instances (`main` + `ahead` run-ahead, `Emulator.h:34,37`).

```cpp
VAmiga();   // VAmiga.h:1673  — constructs everything
~VAmiga();  // VAmiga.h:1674
```

Web glue pattern (`main.cpp:751-759`): `this->emu = new VAmiga();` inside a `vAmigaWrapper`. The raw `Emulator*` is reachable as `vamiga.amiga.emu` (the `API::emu` member, `VAmiga.h:25`) — web code calls `emu->emu->computeFrame()`, `emu->emu->update()`, `emu->emu->getTexture()` directly on it.

Sub-APIs are **public member objects** of `VAmiga` (not pointers you allocate) — `VAmiga.h:1619-1653`:
- Components: `amiga` (AmigaAPI), `agnus`, `ciaA`, `ciaB`, `cpu`, `denise`, `mem` (MemoryAPI), `paula`, `rtc`
- Ports: `audioPort`, `controlPort1`, `controlPort2`, `serialPort`, `videoPort`
- Peripherals: `df0..df3` (+ array `df[4]`), `hd0..hd3` (+ `hd[4]`), `keyboard`
- Misc: `msgQueue`, `recorder`, `remoteManager`, `retroShell`, `debugger`
- Static: `static DefaultsAPI defaults;` (`VAmiga.h:1619`) — shared across instances.

Each sub-API derives from `class API` (`VAmiga.h:21-32`) which holds `Emulator *emu` and exposes `suspend()`/`resume()`. Nested sub-objects: `controlPort1.mouse` (MouseAPI), `controlPort1.joystick` (JoystickAPI), `agnus.copper/blitter/dmaDebugger`, `cpu.debugger/breakpoints/watchpoints`, `paula.diskController/audioChannel0..3/uart`, `mem.debugger`, `hd0.controller`.

Version: `static string VAmiga::version()` / `VAmiga::build()` (`VAmiga.h:1662,1666`). Core version is 4.3.1, snapshot version 4.3.1 (`config.h:17-26`).

---

## 2. Power / run / reset / step (all on `VAmiga`, `VAmiga.h`)

State enum `vamiga::ExecState` (`Core/Infrastructure/ThreadTypes.h:21-27`): `OFF, PAUSED, RUNNING, HALTED`.

| Method | Signature | Purpose | Thread notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `powerOn()` | `void powerOn()` (1742) | OFF→PAUSED. No-op if already on. | call on your emu thread; web calls it from the JS/main thread followed by `emu->emu->update()` (`main.cpp:1015-1017`) |
| `powerOff()` | `void powerOff()` (1749) | →OFF. Web pattern: `powerOff(); emu->emu->update();` (`main.cpp:1015,1649,1713,1768`) |
| `run()` | `void run()` (1759) | →RUNNING. Implicit `powerOn()` if off. Needed after a `pause()` or the core stays muted (`main.cpp:1717`) |
| `pause()` | `void pause()` (1767) | RUNNING→PAUSED after current frame |
| `hardReset()` | `void hardReset()` (1774) | Power-cycle equivalent. Web: `wrapper->emu->hardReset()` (`main.cpp:1902`) |
| `softReset()` | `void softReset()` (1780) | CPU reset instruction equivalent (`main.cpp:2316`) |
| `halt()` | `void halt()` (1788) | →HALTED (shutdown only; terminal) |
| `warpOn(isize source=0)` / `warpOff(isize source=0)` | (1792/1796) | warp mode (run as fast as possible) |
| `trackOn/trackOff(isize source=0)` | (1800/1804) | instruction-track mode (debugger) |
| `stepInto()` | `void stepInto()` (1818) | single instruction; only when PAUSED |
| `stepOver()` | `void stepOver()` (1839) | step over JSR etc.; only when PAUSED |
| `finishLine()` | `void finishLine()` (1845) | run to end of rasterline |
| `finishFrame()` | `void finishFrame()` (1851) | run to end of frame |

State queries (all `bool ... const`, `VAmiga.h:1694-1722`): `isPoweredOn, isPoweredOff, isPaused, isRunning, isSuspended, isHalted, isWarping, isTracking`. `void isReady() const` (1729) **throws** `AppError` if ROMs missing — web wraps in try/catch to detect missing-ROM (`main.cpp:767`). Info structs: `getInfo()`/`getCachedInfo()` → `EmulatorInfo` (state bools, `EmulatorTypes.h:443-453`), `getStats()` → `EmulatorStats` (cpuLoad/fps/resyncs/clones, `EmulatorTypes.h:456-463`).

`suspend()`/`resume()` (`API::suspend/resume`, `VAmiga.h:30-31`; impl `Thread.cpp`) — RAII-style nesting via `suspendCounter`. Use around config changes while running.

---

## 3. Execution entry points (the loop YOU must drive)

On the `Emulator` (reach via `vamiga.amiga.emu`, type `Emulator*`):
- `void computeFrame()` (`Emulator.h:125`) — **computes exactly one frame**. This is your per-frame tick.
- `void update()` (`Emulator.h:122`) — drains the command queue (`put()` commands) and applies pending state changes / config. Web calls `emu->emu->update()` after every input and once per host frame (`main.cpp:420`, and after every `wasm_key`/config change). **Call `update()` before `computeFrame()` each tick, and after any `put()`/config write.**
- `void launch(const void *listener, Callback *func)` (`Emulator.h:59`, exposed as `VAmiga::launch`, `VAmiga.h:1887`) — calls `initialize()` and registers the message callback. Must be called once during init. Web: `wrapper->emu->launch(wrapper->emu, &theListener)` (`main.cpp:860`).

`Callback` signature: `typedef void Callback(const void *, Message);` (`Core/Infrastructure/MsgQueueTypes.h:289`). The `listener` pointer is passed back as arg 1 — pass your bridge `self`. Web's listener: `void theListener(const void *emu, Message msg)` (`main.cpp:654`).

Frame-pacing reference (`main.cpp:402-579`): compute target frame count from elapsed wall time × `target_fps` × `speed_boost`; PAL=50, NTSC=60 (`main.cpp:65-67`); execute the delta via `computeFrame()`, capped at `MAX_GAP=5` (`main.cpp:389,449`). Warp: while `isWarping()` true, burst up to 25 `computeFrame()` calls (`main.cpp:423-436`).

---

## 4. Configuration

### Machine model presets
`enum class vamiga::ConfigScheme : long` (`Core/Components/AmigaTypes.h:154-160`): `A1000_OCS_1MB, A500_OCS_1MB, A500_ECS_1MB, A500_PLUS_1MB`. **No A2000 preset** — for A2000 you configure individual `Opt`s (bankmap, Agnus rev, RAM). Apply: `void VAmiga::set(ConfigScheme model)` (`VAmiga.h:1921`). RetroShell equivalent: `amiga init A500_OCS_1MB` (`Headless.cpp:296-299`).

### Option setters/getters (`VAmiga.h:1903-1946`)
```cpp
i64  get(Opt option) const;                 // 1903
i64  get(Opt option, long id) const;        // 1915  (id = component index, e.g. drive nr)
void set(Opt opt, i64 value);               // 1933
void set(Opt opt, i64 value, long id);      // 1946
```
Note `Emulator::set` takes `std::vector<isize> objids` (`Emulator.h:104`); the VAmiga facade `set(Opt,i64,long id)` wraps single-id, but web also calls `emu->set(Opt::DRIVE_CONNECT, true, {1})` style with brace-init id (`main.cpp:799`). Use `set(opt, value, driveNr)`.

### Key `Opt` values (`enum class vamiga::Opt`, `Core/Infrastructure/OptionTypes.h`)
- `Opt::AMIGA_VIDEO_FORMAT` (line 29) — PAL/NTSC (enum `VideoFormat` in AmigaTypes)
- `Opt::AGNUS_REVISION` (45) — `AgnusRevision` enum: `OCS_OLD, OCS, ECS_1MB, ECS_2MB`
- `Opt::DENISE_REVISION` (49) — `OCS, ECS`
- `Opt::CPU_REVISION` (125) — `68000, 68010, 68EC020`
- `Opt::RTC_MODEL` (133) — `NONE, OKI, RICOH`
- `Opt::MEM_CHIP_RAM` (136) — KB (256/512/1024)
- `Opt::MEM_SLOW_RAM` (137) — KB (0/512)
- `Opt::MEM_FAST_RAM` (138) — KB (0..8192)
- `Opt::MEM_EXT_START` (139), `MEM_SLOW_RAM_DELAY/MIRROR` (141/142), `MEM_BANKMAP` (143: `A500/A1000/A2000A/A2000B`)
- `Opt::DRIVE_CONNECT` (153, per-drive id), `Opt::DRIVE_TYPE` (`DD_35`/`HD_35`, `main.cpp:1562`)
- `Opt::HDC_CONNECT` (165, per-controller id)
- `Opt::AUD_VOLL` / `AUD_VOLR` (208/209) — master volume 0..100
- `Opt::DC_SPEED` (drive controller speed; `-1` for EmuTOS, `main.cpp:1778`)

Reference init sequence (`main.cpp:778-801`):
```cpp
emu->defaults.defaults->setFallback(Opt::HDC_CONNECT, false, {0});
emu->set(Opt::AUD_VOLL, 100);  emu->set(Opt::AUD_VOLR, 100);
emu->set(Opt::MEM_CHIP_RAM, 512);  emu->set(Opt::MEM_SLOW_RAM, 512);
emu->set(Opt::AGNUS_REVISION, (i64)AgnusRevision::OCS);
emu->set(Opt::HDC_CONNECT, false, {0});
emu->set(Opt::DRIVE_CONNECT, true, {1});   // connect df1
emu->emu->update();
```

### Kickstart ROM (on `mem`, MemoryAPI, `VAmiga.h:509-535`)
```cpp
void loadRom(const fs::path &path);     // 512   auto-detects ROM type
void loadRom(MediaFile &file);          // 517
void loadRom(const u8 *buf, isize len); // 522   ← use this for in-memory bytes
void loadExt(...);                      // ext/Action-Replay ROM, same 3 overloads
void saveRom(const fs::path&); deleteRom();   // 527/533
const RomTraits &getRomTraits() const;  // 501   .title, .released, etc.
```
Web loads from a `RomFile`: `RomFile *rom = new RomFile(blob,len); wrapper->emu->mem.loadRom(*rom);` (`main.cpp:1757,1772`). Headless loads embedded bytes: `vamiga.mem.loadRom(diagROM13, sizeofDiagRom13)` (`Headless.cpp:142`). ROM must be flashed **while powered off** — web does `powerOff(); update(); loadRom(); ... powerOn(); run();` (`main.cpp:1766-1813`). `config.h:46` sets `ALLOW_ALL_ROMS = 1` (magic-byte check disabled).

---

## 5. Snapshot / save-state (on `amiga`, AmigaAPI, `VAmiga.h:75-93`)

```cpp
class MediaFile *takeSnapshot();                  // 75  caller OWNS the returned object → must delete
void loadSnapshot(const MediaFile &snapshot);     // 81
void loadSnapshot(const fs::path &path);          // 82
void saveSnapshot(const fs::path &path) const;    // 88
void loadWorkspace(const fs::path&); saveWorkspace(const fs::path&) const;  // 92/93 (experimental)
```

`MediaFile` is the abstract media base; concrete snapshot type is `vamiga::Snapshot` (header `Core/Media/.../Snapshot.h`, included in `main.cpp:21`). Take + serialize (`main.cpp:1257-1281`):
```cpp
MediaFile *snapshot = wrapper->emu->amiga.takeSnapshot();
u8 *data = (u8*)(((Snapshot*)snapshot)->getHeader());  // raw bytes incl. header
isize size = snapshot->getSize();
auto [w,h] = snapshot->previewImageSize();             // preview thumbnail dims
// ... persist `data`[0..size) ...  then delete snapshot
```
Load from bytes (`main.cpp:1722-1731`): `Snapshot *file = new Snapshot(blob, len); wrapper->emu->amiga.loadSnapshot(*file); delete file;`. `Snapshot::isCompatible(blob,len)` (`main.cpp:1722`) validates magic/version. Messages `Msg::SNAPSHOT_TAKEN` / `SNAPSHOT_RESTORED` (`MsgQueueTypes.h:116-117`) fire on the callback. Snapshot version mismatch throws (`FORCE_SNAP_TOO_OLD/NEW/IS_BETA/CORRUPTED` faults, `config.h:225-228`).

`MediaFile` also factories disk/ROM/HDF files. Concrete file classes (all in `Core/Media/`, included `main.cpp:14-25`): `RomFile, ADFFile, ADZFile, DMSFile, EXEFile, HDFFile, HDZFile, EADFFile, STFile, OtherFile, Snapshot`. Each has `static bool isCompatible(...)` and ctors `(const u8*, isize)` or `(path)`.

---

## 6. Video framebuffer (the render source)

`VideoPortAPI` (`VAmiga.h:1090-1137`) — preferred host path:
```cpp
void lockTexture();                                 // 1115  grab mutex BEFORE reading
void unlockTexture();                               // 1122  release after copy/upload
const u32 *getTexture() const;                      // 1130
const u32 *getTexture(isize *nr, bool *lof, bool *prevlof) const;  // 1131
void findInnerArea(isize &x1,&x2,&y1,&y2) const;    // 1135 visible viewport
void findInnerAreaNormalized(double&...) const;     // 1136
```

Underlying buffer = `vamiga::Texture` (`Core/Components/Denise/Texture.h:22`): fields `i64 nr`, `Buffer<Texel> pixels`, `bool lof`, `bool prevlof`. Pixel pointer is `getTexture().pixels.ptr` (web uses `emu->emu->getTexture().pixels.ptr` directly, `main.cpp:874`).

**Pixel format & dimensions:**
- `Texel == u32` when `TPP==1` (`config.h:38`, `FrameBufferTypes.h:30`). Layout is RGBA where **R is the low byte**: `Texture::red = TEXEL(0xFF0000FF)` → A=0xFF, B=0x00, G=0x00, R=0xFF in the literal, i.e. memory order R,G,B,A (little-endian u32 = 0xAABBGGRR). For Metal use `MTLPixelFormatRGBA8Unorm` reading the u32 as little-endian, or `bgra8Unorm` with channel swizzle — verify against the apple2ts reference per MEMORY.md. (TPP=2 would make Texel `u64`/super-hires; this build is TPP=1.)
- Full texture is **`HPIXELS × VPIXELS` = `912 × 313`** texels (`Constants.h:89-91`; `PIXELS = 912*313`). `getTexture()` in web is offset by `HBLANK_MIN*4*TPP = 0x12*4 = 72` to skip left HBLANK (`main.cpp:561`).
- The doc comment at `VAmiga.h:1126` references `vamiga::Texture::width`/`height` constants, **but those are not defined** in `Texture.h` in this tree — use `HPIXELS`/`VPIXELS` from `Constants.h`.
- Visible/clipped region is dynamic (viewport tracking). Web defaults: clip ~`724×568` (`main.cpp:87-88`), or computed from `Msg::VIEWPORT` (`main.cpp:74`, handled in `theListener` at `main.cpp:657-690`, fields `viewport.hstrt/vstrt/hstop/vstop`). For a first cut, render the full `912×313` and crop with a fixed viewport (mirror apple2ts framing).
- Frame info bits (web `wasm_frame_info`, `main.cpp:877-892`): `nr<<2 | (prevlof?2:0) | (lof?1:0)`. `lof`/`prevlof` = long-frame flags for interlace de-jitter.

**Threading:** `lockTexture()`/`unlockTexture()` guard `Emulator::textureLock` (`Emulator.h:46,172-173`). Since you drive `computeFrame()` on your emu thread, the renderer (Metal, main/CADisplayLink thread) must `lockTexture()` → memcpy/upload → `unlockTexture()`. Or, simpler given single-thread fork: copy the texture on the emu thread right after `computeFrame()` into a double-buffer the renderer reads.

---

## 7. Audio (on `audioPort`, AudioPortAPI, `VAmiga.h:975-1037`)

```cpp
isize copyMono(float *buffer, isize n);                 // 1006
isize copyStereo(float *left, float *right, isize n);   // 1014  ← web uses this
isize copyInterleaved(float *buffer, isize n);          // 1025
const AudioPortStats &getStats() const;                 // 992
```
Pull model — drain into your `AVAudioEngine`/`AURemoteIO` render callback. Web (`main.cpp:1300-1314`): query available count via the raw port `audioPort.port->stream.count()`, then `copyStereo(left, right, 1024)` in chunks. Samples are `float`. There is no fixed sample-rate getter exposed here (configured via `Opt` audio options; default ~44.1k). The bridge should pull on the audio thread, not the emu thread.

---

## 8. Input

### Keyboard (`keyboard`, KeyboardAPI, `VAmiga.h:851-900`)
`KeyCode = u8` (`Core/Infrastructure/Aliases.h:35`) — Amiga raw key code, NOT ASCII.
```cpp
void press(KeyCode key, double delay=0.0, double duration=0.0);  // 878
void release(KeyCode key, double delay=0.0);                     // 891
void toggle(KeyCode key, double delay=0.0, double duration=0.0); // 885
void releaseAll();                                               // 895
bool isPressed(KeyCode key) const;                               // 871
void abortTyping();                                              // 899
```
Web keyDown/Up (`main.cpp:924-937`): `keyboard.keyboard->press(KeyCode(code))` / `keyboard.release(KeyCode(code))`, then `emu->emu->update()`. (Web reaches the raw `Keyboard*` for press but uses the API for release — either works; prefer `keyboard.press/release`.) Alternative async path via command queue: `put(Cmd::KEY_PRESS, KeyCmd{keycode,delay})` / `Cmd::KEY_RELEASE` / `Cmd::KEY_RELEASE_ALL` (`CmdQueueTypes.h:62-64,174-179`).

### Mouse (`controlPort1.mouse` / `controlPort2.mouse`, MouseAPI, `VAmiga.h:907-963`)
```cpp
void setXY(double x, double y);          // 951  absolute
void setDxDy(double dx, double dy);      // 957  relative
void trigger(GamePadAction action);      // 962  buttons
bool detectShakeXY/DxDy(...);            // 932/945
```
Web routes mouse through the **command queue** (preferred, thread-safe) rather than direct calls (`main.cpp:1944-1968`):
```cpp
emu->put(Cmd::MOUSE_MOVE_REL, CoordCmd{port-1, x, y});                    // move
emu->put(Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON,   GamePadCmd{port-1, GamePadAction::PRESS_LEFT});   // / RELEASE_LEFT, PRESS_MIDDLE, PRESS_RIGHT
```
`Cmd::MOUSE_MOVE_ABS/REL/BUTTON` (`CmdQueueTypes.h:68-70`). Port index is **0-based** in commands (`port-1`).

### Joystick (`controlPort1.joystick` / `controlPort2.joystick`, JoystickAPI, `VAmiga.h:825-844`)
```cpp
void trigger(GamePadAction event);   // 843
```
`enum class vamiga::GamePadAction : long` (`Core/Peripherals/Joystick/JoystickTypes.h:31-52`): `PULL_UP=0, PULL_DOWN, PULL_LEFT, PULL_RIGHT, PRESS_FIRE, PRESS_FIRE2, PRESS_FIRE3, PRESS_LEFT, PRESS_MIDDLE, PRESS_RIGHT, RELEASE_X, RELEASE_Y, RELEASE_XY, RELEASE_FIRE, RELEASE_FIRE2, RELEASE_FIRE3, RELEASE_LEFT, RELEASE_MIDDLE, RELEASE_RIGHT`. Web (`main.cpp:2050-2056`): `controlPort1.joystick.trigger(code)`. D-pad: pull on touch-down, `RELEASE_X`/`RELEASE_Y`/`RELEASE_XY` on release. Fire button: `PRESS_FIRE`/`RELEASE_FIRE`.

## 9. Disk insert / eject (`df0..df3`, FloppyDriveAPI, `VAmiga.h:641-706`)

```cpp
void insertMedia(MediaFile &file, bool wp);            // 688
void insert(const fs::path &path, bool wp);            // 689
void insertBlankDisk(FSFormat, BootBlockId, string name, path={});  // 682
void insertFileSystem(const MutableFileSystem &fs, bool wp);        // 695
void ejectDisk();                                       // 699
MediaFile *exportDisk(FileType type);                   // 701
class FloppyDisk &getDisk() const;                      // 661
bool isInsertable(Diameter t, Density d) const;         // 674
const FloppyDriveInfo &getInfo() const;                 // 655
```
Web uses a lower-level `swapDisk` path it reaches via the raw `FloppyDrive*` (`main.cpp:1569,1579-1583`): it builds a `std::unique_ptr<FloppyDisk>` from the file (`load_disk()`, `main.cpp:1480-1528`, dispatching on `ADFFile/ADZFile/DMSFile/EADFFile/EXEFile/STFile/OtherFile::isCompatible`), then `df0.drive->swapDisk(std::move(disk))`. **For the iOS bridge prefer the public API**: build an `ADFFile adf{blob,len}` (or detect type) and call `df0.insertMedia(adf, /*wp*/false)` — cleaner than reaching into `.drive`. df0 only accepts DD (`Density::DD`); df1-df3 accept DD+HD — set `Opt::DRIVE_TYPE` to `HD_35`/`DD_35` per drive (`main.cpp:1562`). Eject: `wrapper->emu->df0.ejectDisk()` (`main.cpp:1027`). Messages: `Msg::DISK_INSERT/DISK_EJECT/DISK_PROTECTED`, `DRIVE_LED/MOTOR/STEP` (`MsgQueueTypes.h:80-90`).

### Hard drive (`hd0..hd3`, HardDriveAPI, `VAmiga.h:734-818`)
`attach(path)` / `attach(MediaFile&)` / `attach(c,h,s,b)` (788-804), `format(FSFormat, name)` (786), `createHDF()` → `MediaFile*` (817), `importFiles(path)` (809). Connect via `Opt::HDC_CONNECT, true, {driveNr}` while powered off, then powerOn/run (`main.cpp:1713-1718`).

---

## 10. Message queue / callback contract

`MsgQueueAPI` (`VAmiga.h:1149-1175`): `bool getMsg(Message &msg)` (poll), `lockMsgQueue()/unlockMsgQueue()`, `string getPayload(isize index)` (WASM extra data). `AmigaAPI::getMsg(Message&)` also exists (`VAmiga.h:122`).

`Message` struct (`Core/Infrastructure/MsgQueueTypes.h:266-282`): `Msg type;` + union `{ struct{i64 value,value2;}; CpuMsg cpu; DriveMsg drive; HdcMsg hdc; ScriptMsg script; SnapshotMsg snapshot; ViewportMsg viewport; }`. Sub-structs at `MsgQueueTypes.h:259-264`: `DriveMsg{i16 nr,value,volume,pan}`, `ViewportMsg{i16 hstrt,vstrt,hstop,vstop}`, `SnapshotMsg{void* snapshot}`, etc.

`enum class vamiga::Msg : long` (`MsgQueueTypes.h:20`+): notable values — `CONFIG, POWER, RUN, PAUSE, STEP, RESET, SHUTDOWN, ABORT, WARP, TRACK, MUTE, POWER_LED_ON/DIM/OFF, VIDEO_FORMAT, CPU_HALT, EOF_REACHED, VIEWPORT, MEM_LAYOUT, DRIVE_CONNECT/SELECT/READ/WRITE/LED/MOTOR/STEP, DISK_INSERT/EJECT/PROTECTED, HDC_CONNECT/STATE, SNAPSHOT_TAKEN/RESTORED, RECORDING_STARTED/STOPPED, SER_IN/OUT, RSH_UPDATE/ERROR`. There is **no `READY_TO_RUN` or `ROM_MISSING` core message** — web synthesizes those JS-side (`main.cpp:771,1806`); the bridge should detect readiness via `isReady()` (throws on missing ROM).

**Callback dispatch is on whatever thread enqueues/processes the message.** The callback `func(listener, msg)` is invoked from inside the core (during `update()`/`computeFrame()` on your emu thread). Marshal to the main thread before touching UIKit/SwiftUI. Web's `theListener` (`main.cpp:654`) just stashes viewport data and posts to JS.

---

## Bridge wiring checklist (mirrors the IIGS app's EmulatorBridge)

1. **Init (once):** `emu = new VAmiga();` → set fallbacks/`ConfigScheme`/`Opt`s → `emu->launch(self, &bridgeCallback);`
2. **ROM:** while powered off, `emu->mem.loadRom(buf,len);` then `try { emu->isReady(); } catch { post ROM_MISSING }`.
3. **Run loop (background pthread you own):** loop { `emu->amiga.emu->update();` `emu->amiga.emu->computeFrame();` pace to 50/60 Hz per §3 }. Use `isWarping()` to burst.
4. **Render (display-link thread):** `videoPort.lockTexture();` upload `videoPort.getTexture()` (912×313 RGBA u32) to Metal; `unlockTexture();` — or copy on emu thread into a double buffer.
5. **Audio (audio thread):** `audioPort.copyStereo(l,r,n)`.
6. **Input:** keyboard `keyboard.press/release(KeyCode)`; mouse/joystick via `emu->put(Cmd::..., {port-1, ...})`; follow each input batch with `update()` (or rely on the loop's `update()`).
7. **Pause/resume:** `pause()`/`run()`. **Save state:** `amiga.takeSnapshot()` → serialize `Snapshot::getHeader()`/`getSize()`, then `delete`. **Load:** `new Snapshot(bytes,len)` → `amiga.loadSnapshot(*s)`.
8. **Disk:** `df[n]->insertMedia(file, wp)` / `ejectDisk()`.
9. **Shutdown:** `emu->halt(); delete emu;`

# VIDEO / FRAMEBUFFER — vAmiga core integration map

## 1. The public API you call (everything is on `VAmiga::videoPort`, type `VideoPortAPI`)

Defined in `/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/VAmiga.h:1090-1137`, implemented in `/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/VAmiga.cpp:984-1017`. Access via your `VAmiga` instance member `videoPort` (declared `VAmiga.h:1636`).

```cpp
void          lockTexture();                                          // acquires the texture mutex
void          unlockTexture();                                        // releases it
const u32*    getTexture() const;                                     // pointer to most-recent STABLE frame
const u32*    getTexture(isize *nr, bool *lof, bool *prevlof) const;  // same + frame metadata (USE THIS ONE)
void          findInnerArea(isize &x1,&x2,&y1,&y2) const;             // auto-detect active (non-border) box
void          findInnerAreaNormalized(double &x1,&x2,&y1,&y2) const;  // same, normalized 0..1
```

`getTexture()` returns `(u32*)emu->getTexture().pixels.ptr` — a pointer to the `Buffer<Texel>` backing store of the stable `Texture`. The 3-arg overload (`VAmiga.cpp:1006`) also writes `nr` (frame counter), `lof` (this frame's long-frame bit), `prevlof` (previous frame's). **Use the 3-arg overload** — you need `nr` for dropped/duplicate-frame detection and `lof`/`prevlof` for interlace handling.

The web glue uses the lower-level `emu->emu->getTexture()` directly (`main.cpp:874`, `880`), which is the `Emulator::getTexture()` that `VideoPortAPI` wraps. From a native bridge, call `vamiga.videoPort.getTexture(&nr,&lof,&prevlof)`.

## 2. Buffer dimensions, pixel format, byte order, exact size

Constants in `/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/Infrastructure/Constants.h:89-91`:
```cpp
static const isize VPIXELS = 313;          // rows  (= VPOS_CNT)
static const isize HPIXELS = 912;          // cols  (= 4 * HPOS_CNT, 4 px per DMA cycle)
static const isize PIXELS  = VPIXELS * HPIXELS;   // 285,456 texels
```

**The texture is ALWAYS the full 912 × 313 grid** — the active picture is a sub-rectangle you crop at upload time (see §5). It does not change size with display mode.

Pixel format (`/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/Components/Denise/FrameBufferTypes.h:31`, gated by `TPP`):
- `config.h:38` → `#define TPP 1` → `typedef u32 Texel;` (hires; SuperHires would need TPP=2/u64 — not your case).
- Each texel is built via `TEXEL(HI_HI_LO_LO(0xFF, b, g, r))` (`PixelEngine.cpp:111`). `HI_HI_LO_LO(x,y,z,w) = (x<<24|y<<16|z<<8|w)` (`Utilities/Macros.h:60`), so the u32 is `0xFF_BB_GG_RR` (A in MSB).
- Confirmed by `GpuColor` accessors (`Components/Denise/Colors.h:151-154`): `r()=byte0`, `g()=byte1`, `b()=byte2`, `a()=byte3`.
- **In memory (little-endian iOS/ARM), byte order is R, G, B, A** = RGBA8888. → Metal `MTLPixelFormatRGBA8Unorm`. The web build uploads it verbatim as `gl.RGBA, gl.UNSIGNED_BYTE` (`vAmiga_canvas_gl.js:294`) with no swizzle, confirming RGBA byte order. Alpha is always 0xFF.

**Exact buffer size:** `HPIXELS * VPIXELS * sizeof(u32)` = `912 * 313 * 4` = **1,141,824 bytes** (285,456 texels). Row stride = `HPIXELS * 4` = **3,648 bytes**.

## 3. Double-buffering / ring buffer (the "stable" frame)

`/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/Components/Denise/PixelEngine.h:48-62`:
```cpp
static constexpr isize NUM_TEXTURES = 8;     // ring of 8 Textures
Texture emuTexture[NUM_TEXTURES];
isize   activeBuffer = 0;                     // the WORKING buffer (Denise draws here)
util::Mutex bufferMutex;
```
- Working buffer = `emuTexture[activeBuffer]`. Stable = the previously completed one.
- `getStableBuffer(offset=0)` (`PixelEngine.cpp:200-205`) returns `emuTexture[(activeBuffer + offset - 1 + 8) % 8]` — i.e. **activeBuffer − 1** = last finished frame. (Negative `offset` reaches older frames for the "run-behind" feature.)
- `swapBuffers()` (`PixelEngine.cpp:231-248`) runs at end-of-frame: locks the texture, advances `activeBuffer = (activeBuffer+1)%8`, and stamps the NEW working buffer's metadata: `nr = agnus.pos.frame`, `lof = agnus.pos.lof`, `prevlof = oldBuffer.lof`. The swap is wrapped in `emulator.lockTexture()/unlockTexture()`.

So from the host you never touch the working buffer; `getTexture()` always hands you a completed, stable frame.

## 4. The `Texture` object & metadata (interlace handling)

`/Users/boris/Downloads/GS/Amiga/vAmigaWeb/Core/Components/Denise/Texture.h:22-62`:
```cpp
class Texture {
    i64            nr = 0;          // frame number (monotonic)
    Buffer<Texel>  pixels;          // the 912*313 u32 array; .ptr = data, .size = PIXELS
    bool           lof = false;     // long-frame bit of THIS frame
    bool           prevlof = false; // long-frame bit of the PREVIOUS frame
    void clear(); ...
};
```
`pixels.ptr` is the raw pointer you upload. `Buffer<Texel>` is `util::Buffer` (`Utilities/Buffer.h`) — `.ptr`/`.size` POD-style access.

**Interlace (long-frame / short-frame) — how the web build deals with it** (`vAmiga_canvas_gl.js:282-328`):
- `lof==true` → "long frame" written into the LF texture; `lof==false` → "short frame" into the SF texture (two separate GL textures, `lfTexture`/`sfTexture`).
- Non-interlace (`currLOF == prevLOF`): both samplers point at the same one texture, weights 1.0 — just show the frame.
- Interlace (`currLOF != prevLOF`): a merge shader blends LF+SF (one is the odd field, one the even). `flicker_weight` (default 1.0; set 0.5–0.6 to emulate interlace flicker) controls blending of the alternating fields. **For a v1 native renderer you can ignore interlace and just upload the single stable frame** — it renders correctly for all non-interlaced (the vast majority of) modes. Add field-merge later if you want flicker-free hi-res-laced.
- The `info` word from `wasm_frame_info()` (`main.cpp:877-893`) packs it: `info = (nr<<2) | (prevlof<<1) | lof`. JS unpacks (`vAmiga_canvas_gl.js:262-266`): `currLOF=info&1; prevLOF=(info>>1)&1; frameNr=info>>2`. With the native `getTexture(&nr,&lof,&prevlof)` you get these directly — no bit-packing needed.

## 5. The per-tick fetch sequence (mirror this in your Metal draw loop)

Reference WASM/WebGL path (`vAmiga_canvas_gl.js:258-296`) and the `main.cpp` C exports (`871-893`):

```
1. (optionally) videoPort.lockTexture();
2. const u32 *px = videoPort.getTexture(&nr, &lof, &prevlof);
3. if (nr == lastNr) skip;            // duplicate frame, nothing new
   if (nr != lastNr+1) ...;           // a frame was dropped (just resync, still draw)
   lastNr = nr;
4. Upload sub-rect [xOff, yOff, clipped_width, clipped_height] from px
   with row stride = HPIXELS (3648 bytes) into your Metal texture.
5. videoPort.unlockTexture();
6. vamiga.wakeUp();                    // tell the core to compute the next frame (see below)
```

The web build computes the source pointer as `wasm_pixel_buffer() + yOff*(HPIXELS<<2)` and uploads a sub-image using `UNPACK_ROW_LENGTH=HPIXELS`, `UNPACK_SKIP_PIXELS=xOff`, width=`clipped_width`, height=`clipped_height` (`vAmiga_canvas_gl.js:278,291-294`). In Metal: create an `MTLTexture` sized `HPIXELS × VPIXELS` (or `clipped_width × clipped_height`) `RGBA8Unorm`; `replaceRegion` with `bytesPerRow = HPIXELS*4 = 3648` and the source offset `px + yOff*HPIXELS + xOff`. Easiest v1: upload the entire 912×313 texture (`bytesPerRow=3648`) and crop with texture coordinates / a viewport in the shader.

**Locking:** `lockTexture()`/`unlockTexture()` guard against the emulator thread doing a `swapBuffers()` mid-read. Since your emulator runs on a background pthread and you read on the render thread, **wrap the read+upload in lock/unlock** (the web single-thread build skips it; you must not). The lock is a `util::Mutex` (`PixelEngine.h:62`, plumbed through `Emulator::lockTexture` per `PixelEngine.cpp:234,247`).

## 6. Default crop rectangle (what the active picture is)

`main.cpp:97-100` — the "STANDARD"/default offsets:
```cpp
int xOff = 12;   // + eat_border_width(0)
int yOff = 12;   // + eat_border_height(0)
int clipped_width  = HPIXELS*TPP - 12 - 24 = 912 - 36 = 876;   // wide; trimmed further per geometry
int clipped_height = VPOS_MAX  - 12 - 24;                       // ~248 visible lines
```
`clip_width = 724*TPP = 724`, `clip_height = 568` (`main.cpp:87-88`) are the "natural" 4:3-ish target. Standard Amiga visible area is ≈ **724 × 568** (PAL, doubled height) or per-mode. For DISPLAY_ADAPTIVE/BORDERLESS the core auto-detects the box via `calculate_viewport_dimensions()` (`main.cpp:191`) or you can call `videoPort.findInnerArea(x1,x2,y1,y2)` (`VideoPort.cpp:151-187`) which shrinks a box by comparing against a border reference pixel and returns the active rectangle in texture coords. `findInnerAreaNormalized` returns the same as 0..1 fractions (divides x by HPIXELS=912, y by VPIXELS=313). **Recommendation:** start with a fixed crop (`xOff=12, yOff=12`, width≈876→clamp to taste, height≈translated), or call `findInnerArea` once after the picture stabilizes, like the web build calibrates every 700ms (`main.cpp:556-559`).

Aspect: Amiga pixels are non-square. PAL hires ≈ 1:1 after the standard crop maps to a 4:3 display; the web build scales the canvas (`scaleVMCanvas`) rather than baking aspect into the texture. Height is effectively doubled on display (`vAmiga_canvas.js:65` uses `clipped_height*2`) because the texture stores one field's worth of lines per frame.

## 7. Frame pacing — `wakeUp()` and where the frame is produced

- The core computes frames on its own thread. After you grab+upload the current texture, call `VAmiga::wakeUp()` (`VAmiga.h:1858-1866`): "send a wakeup signal on each VSYNC pulse… right after the current texture has been handed over to the GPU." This is the standard host→core pacing handshake — call it once per display refresh (CADisplayLink), after the Metal upload.
- The headless/worker path instead drives frames explicitly: `emu->emu->computeFrame()` (`main.cpp:543`) / `emu->execute()` (`main.cpp:822`). For a UI app, prefer `run()` + `wakeUp()` pacing over manual `computeFrame()`.
- Dropped-frame accounting lives in `VideoPort::buffersWillSwap()` (`VideoPort.cpp:135-147`): compares `latestGrabbedFrame` (set inside `getTexture`, `VideoPort.cpp:119`) vs current `nr`; if you fall behind it increments `stats.droppedFrames`. Not something you must act on — just be aware grabbing the texture updates that watermark.

## 8. De-noise / power-off behavior

`VideoPort::getTexture()` (`VideoPort.cpp:113-133`):
- Powered on → returns the real stable buffer.
- Powered off + `VID_WHITE_NOISE` option on → returns a `whiteNoise` texture (random walk over a precomputed noise buffer, `VideoPort.cpp:122-129`).
- Otherwise → `blank` (solid `0xFF000000` black, `VideoPort.cpp:31-33`).

So the pointer is always valid and always full-size even before a ROM/disk is running — your renderer can upload unconditionally. There is **no separate de-noise/merge pass on the core side**; the only "merge" is the optional interlace field-blend, which is done GPU-side in the web shader, not in the core.

## 9. Minimal native bridge surface for video

```cpp
// EmulatorBridge (Obj-C++), called from the render thread / CADisplayLink:
const uint32_t *frame(int64_t *outNr, bool *outLof, bool *outPrevLof) {
    vamiga.videoPort.lockTexture();                       // guard vs emu thread swap
    isize nr; bool lof, prevlof;
    const u32 *p = vamiga.videoPort.getTexture(&nr,&lof,&prevlof);
    *outNr = nr; *outLof = lof; *outPrevLof = prevlof;
    return p;   // caller must finish reading then call unlock()
}
void unlockFrame() { vamiga.videoPort.unlockTexture(); }
void hostVSync()   { vamiga.wakeUp(); }                   // after upload

// Exposed constants for Swift/Metal:
//   width  = 912  (HPIXELS)   height = 313 (VPIXELS)
//   bytesPerRow = 3648        totalBytes = 1,141,824
//   pixelFormat = RGBA8Unorm
//   default crop: x=12, y=12, w≈876, h≈248  (or call findInnerArea once)
```

Key file:line anchors: API decl `VAmiga.h:1090-1137`; API impl `VAmiga.cpp:984-1017`; Texture struct `Components/Denise/Texture.h:22-62`; ring buffer + swap `Components/Denise/PixelEngine.{h:48-62, cpp:200-248}`; pixel format `Components/Denise/FrameBufferTypes.h:31`, `Components/Denise/PixelEngine.cpp:111`, `Components/Denise/Colors.h:151-154`, `Utilities/Macros.h:60`; dimensions `Infrastructure/Constants.h:89-91`; `TPP` `config.h:38`; web fetch/upload glue `main.cpp:871-893` and `js/vAmiga_canvas_gl.js:258-328`; crop defaults `main.cpp:87-100`; auto-crop `Ports/VideoPort.cpp:151-199`.

# vAmiga Core — INPUT INJECTION Engineering Map

All paths verified against `Core/VAmiga.h`, the type headers, the core component classes, and the WASM glue in `main.cpp`. Namespace is `vamiga::` for everything.

## 0. Two injection styles (both valid, both used in main.cpp)

The core exposes **two equivalent ways** to inject input:

1. **Direct API calls** — reach into the component object and call its method synchronously, e.g. `vamiga.keyboard.press(...)`, `vamiga.controlPort1.mouse->setDxDy(...)`, `vamiga.controlPort1.joystick.trigger(...)`. These execute on the calling thread.
2. **Command queue** — `vamiga.put(Cmd::..., payload)`. The command is enqueued and drained by the emulator thread inside `Amiga::process(...)` (the dispatch switch is at `Core/Components/Amiga.cpp:903-991`). This is the thread-safe path.

`main.cpp` mixes both: keyboard uses **direct** (`keyboard.press/release`), mouse uses **queue** (`Cmd::MOUSE_MOVE_REL`, `Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON`), joystick uses **direct** (`joystick.trigger`). For a native app driving the core on a background pthread, **prefer the command-queue path** (`put(...)`) for everything — it is the thread-safe contract; the direct calls are only safe if you call them on the emulator thread or while paused/suspended.

`KeyCode` = **`typedef u8 KeyCode;`** (`Core/Infrastructure/Aliases.h:35`). It is the **raw Amiga keycode** (0x00–0x7F), NOT an ASCII/USB/HID code. No internal translation happens — whatever byte you pass is the hardware keycode put on the Amiga keyboard serial line. The host is responsible for mapping platform keys → Amiga raw keycodes.

---

## 1. KEYBOARD

### Public API (`Core/VAmiga.h:851-900`, class `KeyboardAPI`, instance `VAmiga::keyboard`)

```cpp
bool isPressed(KeyCode key) const;
void press(KeyCode key, double delay = 0.0, double duration = 0.0);
void toggle(KeyCode key, double delay = 0.0, double duration = 0.0);
void release(KeyCode key, double delay = 0.0);
void releaseAll();
void abortTyping();
```
- `press`: if `duration > 0`, the key auto-releases after `duration` seconds. `delay` schedules the press `delay` seconds in the future. For an iOS keyDown/keyUp model, call `press(code)` on keyDown and `release(code)` on keyUp, both with default 0 args.
- Underlying core class `Keyboard` (`Core/Peripherals/Keyboard/Keyboard.h:150-160`): `press(KeyCode)`, `release(KeyCode)`, `toggle(KeyCode)`, `releaseAll()`, `isPressed(KeyCode)`, plus `autoType(const string&)` for pasting text. 10-entry type-ahead ring buffer; `bool keyDown[128]` tracks held keys.

### Command-queue path (thread-safe, preferred)
```cpp
// KeyCmd = { KeyCode keycode; double delay; }  (CmdQueueTypes.h:174-179)
vamiga.put(Cmd::KEY_PRESS,   KeyCmd{ code, 0.0 });
vamiga.put(Cmd::KEY_RELEASE, KeyCmd{ code, 0.0 });
vamiga.put(Cmd::KEY_RELEASE_ALL);
vamiga.put(Cmd::KEY_TOGGLE,  KeyCmd{ code, 0.0 });
```
Dispatched at `Amiga.cpp:941-947` → `keyboard.processCommand(cmd)`. Note: `VAmiga::put` has a typed overload `put(Cmd type, KeyCmd payload)` (`VAmiga.h:1971`). The `Cmd` enum comments say "C64 keyboard" — copy-paste leftover; it is the Amiga keyboard.

### main.cpp call pattern (verified, lines 924-939)
```cpp
extern "C" void wasm_key(int code, int pressed) {
  if (pressed == 1)
      wrapper->emu->keyboard.keyboard->press(KeyCode(code));  // direct, reaches inner core obj
  else
      wrapper->emu->keyboard.release(KeyCode(code));          // direct, via API wrapper
  wrapper->emu->emu->update();   // drains internal state / command queue
}
```
The browser side already converted the key to a raw Amiga keycode before calling `wasm_key`; `code` is passed straight through as `KeyCode`. `emu->update()` is `Emulator::update()` — call it after enqueuing/injecting so the change is processed promptly.

### THE KEY MAP (browser `code` → Amiga raw keycode)
From `js/vAmiga_keyboard.js:168-283` (`key_translation_map`). This is the canonical browser-`KeyboardEvent.code` → Amiga raw `KeyCode` table the host must replicate. For an iOS app you map `UIKey`/`UIKeyboardHIDUsage` (or your on-screen key identifiers) to these same Amiga codes.

Amiga raw keycodes (hex):

---

## 2. MOUSE

### Public API (`Core/VAmiga.h:907-963`, class `MouseAPI`, instances `VAmiga::controlPort1.mouse` and `controlPort2.mouse`)

The mouse lives **under a control port**: `ControlPortAPI` (`VAmiga.h:1045-1059`) contains `JoystickAPI joystick;` and `MouseAPI mouse;`. The top-level `VAmiga` has `ControlPortAPI controlPort1, controlPort2;` (`VAmiga.h:1633-1634`). Port 1 is the rear "mouse" port, port 2 is typically the joystick port — but either can host either device.

```cpp
void setXY(double x, double y);       // ABSOLUTE position
void setDxDy(double dx, double dy);   // RELATIVE delta (most useful for touch drags)
void trigger(GamePadAction action);   // button events (see enum below)
bool detectShakeXY(double x, double y);
bool detectShakeDxDy(double dx, double dy);
```
Underlying core class `Mouse` (`Core/Peripherals/Mouse/Mouse.h:237-246`): `setXY`, `setDxDy`, `setLeftButton(bool)`, `setMiddleButton(bool)`, `setRightButton(bool)`, `trigger(GamePadAction)`. Public button flags `leftButton/middleButton/rightButton`. There is also `Opt::MOUSE_VELOCITY` (scaling), `Opt::MOUSE_PULLUP_RESISTORS`, `Opt::MOUSE_SHAKE_DETECTION`.

### Buttons use the GamePadAction enum (NOT raw bools, when going through the API/queue)
`trigger(...)` and the `MOUSE_BUTTON` command take a `GamePadAction`. Relevant mouse values (`JoystickTypes.h:31-52`):
- `GamePadAction::PRESS_LEFT` / `RELEASE_LEFT`
- `GamePadAction::PRESS_MIDDLE` / `RELEASE_MIDDLE`
- `GamePadAction::PRESS_RIGHT` / `RELEASE_RIGHT`

```cpp
// CoordCmd   = { isize port; double x; double y; }   (CmdQueueTypes.h:181-187)
// GamePadCmd = { isize port; GamePadAction action; }  (CmdQueueTypes.h:189-194)
// port is 0-based: 0 = controlPort1, 1 = controlPort2

vamiga.put(Cmd::MOUSE_MOVE_REL, CoordCmd{ port, dx, dy });   // relative
vamiga.put(Cmd::MOUSE_MOVE_ABS, CoordCmd{ port, x,  y  });   // absolute
vamiga.put(Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON,   GamePadCmd{ port, GamePadAction::PRESS_LEFT });
vamiga.put(Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON,   GamePadCmd{ port, GamePadAction::RELEASE_LEFT });
```
Routing: `Amiga.cpp:949-962` — `cmd.coord.port`/`cmd.action.port` selects `controlPort2` if nonzero else `controlPort1`, then calls `port.processCommand(cmd)` → `ControlPort::processCommand` (`ControlPort.cpp:160-173`) which dispatches `MOUSE_MOVE_REL→mouse.setDxDy`, `MOUSE_MOVE_ABS→mouse.setXY`, `MOUSE_BUTTON→mouse.trigger`.

```cpp
extern "C" void wasm_mouse(int port, int x, int y) {           // x,y are RELATIVE deltas here

  if (button_id==1)
    wrapper->emu->put(Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON, GamePadCmd(port-1, pressed==1 ? GamePadAction::PRESS_LEFT  : GamePadAction::RELEASE_LEFT));
  else if (button_id==2)
    wrapper->emu->put(Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON, GamePadCmd(port-1, pressed==1 ? GamePadAction::PRESS_MIDDLE: GamePadAction::RELEASE_MIDDLE));
  else if (button_id==3)
    wrapper->emu->put(Cmd::MOUSE_BUTTON, GamePadCmd(port-1, pressed==1 ? GamePadAction::PRESS_RIGHT : GamePadAction::RELEASE_RIGHT));
}
```
**Note** the WASM JS passes 1-based ports and main.cpp converts with `port-1` to the 0-based payload. The commented-out direct calls at lines 1948-1951 (`controlPort1.mouse->setDxDy(x,y)`) show the alternative direct path.

### Coordinate semantics
- `setDxDy(dx,dy)` accumulates relative motion — feed raw touch/trackpad deltas (in pixels). Velocity scaling via `Opt::MOUSE_VELOCITY`. This is the right call for finger-drag → mouse-move.
- `setXY(x,y)` is absolute — `x`/`y` are in the emulator's coordinate space (scaled internally by `scaleX/scaleY`). For absolute touch-to-position mapping you must map touch point → texture coords first. Relative (`MOUSE_MOVE_REL`) is simpler and what the web build uses.

---

## 3. JOYSTICK

### Public API (`Core/VAmiga.h:825-844`, class `JoystickAPI`, instances `VAmiga::controlPort1.joystick`, `controlPort2.joystick`)
```cpp
void trigger(GamePadAction event);
const JoystickInfo &getInfo() const;     // { bool button; isize axisX; isize axisY; }
const JoystickConfig &getConfig() const; // autofire settings
```
Underlying core class `Joystick` (`Core/Peripherals/Joystick/Joystick.h:183`): single entry point `void trigger(GamePadAction event)`. State: `bool button/button2/button3`, `isize axisX` (-1 left / +1 right / 0), `isize axisY` (-1 up / +1 down / 0).

### Direction + fire enum — `GamePadAction` (`Core/Peripherals/Joystick/JoystickTypes.h:31-52`)
```cpp
enum class GamePadAction : long {
    PULL_UP = 0,    // joystick up
    PULL_DOWN,      // down
    PULL_LEFT,      // left
    PULL_RIGHT,     // right
    PRESS_FIRE,     // button 1
    PRESS_FIRE2,    // button 2
    PRESS_FIRE3,    // button 3
    PRESS_LEFT,     // (mouse LMB)
    PRESS_MIDDLE,   // (mouse MMB)
    PRESS_RIGHT,    // (mouse RMB)
    RELEASE_X,      // recenter horizontal axis (cancels PULL_LEFT/RIGHT)
    RELEASE_Y,      // recenter vertical axis  (cancels PULL_UP/DOWN)
    RELEASE_XY,     // recenter both axes
    RELEASE_FIRE,   // release button 1
    RELEASE_FIRE2,  // release button 2
    RELEASE_FIRE3,  // release button 3
    RELEASE_LEFT, RELEASE_MIDDLE, RELEASE_RIGHT  // mouse button releases
};
```
Underlying numeric values are the declaration order (0..18) since only `PULL_UP=0` is fixed. The enum is shared by mouse buttons and joystick directions/fire — same type, semantics differ by which device's `trigger` you call.

Direction model is **per-axis**, not 8-way discrete: send `PULL_LEFT` to set axisX=-1; send `RELEASE_X` to return axisX to 0. A diagonal = `PULL_UP` + `PULL_LEFT` (two triggers). To neutralize everything: `RELEASE_XY` then `RELEASE_FIRE`. This maps cleanly to a d-pad: each direction press → `PULL_*`, each direction release → `RELEASE_X`/`RELEASE_Y` (or `RELEASE_XY` on full release).

### Command-queue path (thread-safe — recommended over the direct `trigger` main.cpp uses)
```cpp
// GamePadCmd = { isize port; GamePadAction action; }  port 0-based
vamiga.put(Cmd::JOY_EVENT, GamePadCmd{ port, GamePadAction::PULL_UP });
vamiga.put(Cmd::JOY_EVENT, GamePadCmd{ port, GamePadAction::PRESS_FIRE });
vamiga.put(Cmd::JOY_EVENT, GamePadCmd{ port, GamePadAction::RELEASE_XY });
```
Routing identical to mouse buttons: `Amiga.cpp:956-962` (`Cmd::JOY_EVENT` shares the block with `MOUSE_BUTTON`) → `controlPort{1,2}.processCommand` → `ControlPort.cpp:168` `joystick.trigger(cmd.action.action)`.

### main.cpp call pattern (verified, lines 1970-2058) — uses DIRECT trigger
```cpp
extern "C" void wasm_joystick(char* port_plus_event) {
  char joyport = port_plus_event[0];          // '1' or '2'
  char* event  = port_plus_event+1;           // "PULL_UP", "PRESS_FIRE", "RELEASE_XY", ...
  GamePadAction code;
  if      (strcmp(event,"PULL_UP")==0)     code = GamePadAction::PULL_UP;
  else if (strcmp(event,"PULL_LEFT")==0)   code = GamePadAction::PULL_LEFT;
  else if (strcmp(event,"PULL_DOWN")==0)   code = GamePadAction::PULL_DOWN;
  else if (strcmp(event,"PULL_RIGHT")==0)  code = GamePadAction::PULL_RIGHT;
  else if (strcmp(event,"PRESS_FIRE")==0)  code = GamePadAction::PRESS_FIRE;
  else if (strcmp(event,"PRESS_FIRE2")==0) code = GamePadAction::PRESS_FIRE2;
  else if (strcmp(event,"PRESS_FIRE3")==0) code = GamePadAction::PRESS_FIRE3;
  else if (strcmp(event,"RELEASE_XY")==0)  code = GamePadAction::RELEASE_XY;
  else if (strcmp(event,"RELEASE_X")==0)   code = GamePadAction::RELEASE_X;
  else if (strcmp(event,"RELEASE_Y")==0)   code = GamePadAction::RELEASE_Y;
  else if (strcmp(event,"RELEASE_FIRE")==0)  code = GamePadAction::RELEASE_FIRE;
  else if (strcmp(event,"RELEASE_FIRE2")==0) code = GamePadAction::RELEASE_FIRE2;
  else if (strcmp(event,"RELEASE_FIRE3")==0) code = GamePadAction::RELEASE_FIRE3;
  else return;
  if (joyport=='1') wrapper->emu->controlPort1.joystick.trigger(code);
  else if (joyport=='2') wrapper->emu->controlPort2.joystick.trigger(code);
}
```
Autofire is built in (`Opt::JOY_AUTOFIRE`, `JOY_AUTOFIRE_BULLETS`, `JOY_AUTOFIRE_DELAY`, `JOY_AUTOFIRE_BURSTS`) — enable via `vamiga.set(Opt::JOY_AUTOFIRE, true, portId)` and `PRESS_FIRE` will pulse.

---

## 4. Device selection on a control port — IMPORTANT, no explicit setup needed

A control port holds both a `Mouse` and a `Joystick` object but only **one** is wired to the hardware registers at a time (`ControlPort::device`, type `ControlPortDevice { NONE, MOUSE, JOYSTICK }`, `ControlPortTypes.h:20-25`). Crucially, **device selection is automatic**:
- Any `Mouse` input event calls `port.setDevice(ControlPortDevice::MOUSE)` (`Mouse.cpp:220,232,242,251,260`).
- Any `Joystick` `trigger` calls `port.setDevice(ControlPortDevice::JOYSTICK)` (`Joystick.cpp:290`).

So the host does **not** need to pre-configure which device is on which port — feeding a mouse event auto-selects mouse, feeding a joystick event auto-selects joystick, on that port. `ControlPort::changePotgo`/`changePra`/`joydat` (`ControlPort.cpp:105-158`) read `device` to decide whose state reaches the chips. (There is a manual setter `ControlPort::setDevice(...)` at `ControlPort.h:155` if you ever want to force it.) Convention: put the mouse on port 1, joystick/gamepad on port 2 — but games vary, so expose a port toggle in UI.

---

## 5. Threading / when input takes effect

- `VAmiga::put(const Command&)` enqueues to the internal `CmdQueue`; the emulator thread drains it at frame boundaries inside `Amiga::process(...)` (`Amiga.cpp:903`). This is the **thread-safe** path — safe to call from your UI thread while the emu pthread runs.
- The **direct** API calls (`keyboard.press`, `mouse->setDxDy`, `joystick.trigger`) mutate component state immediately on the calling thread. main.cpp gets away with this because WASM is single-threaded. On a real background-thread architecture, route input through `put(...)` to avoid races, OR call directly only under `api.suspend()/resume()` (every `API` has `suspend()`/`resume()`, `VAmiga.h:30-31`) or while paused.
- After injecting, main.cpp calls `emu->emu->update()` (the inner `Emulator::update()`) to process pending state. With a free-running emu thread that already calls `update()` each frame, you don't strictly need to, but calling it reduces input latency for queued commands. (`vamiga.wakeUp()`, `VAmiga.h:1866`, is the VSYNC pacing signal — unrelated to input, don't confuse the two.)

# vAmiga — DISKS, ROM & MEDIA integration map

Everything lives in `namespace vamiga`. The single host-facing facade is `class VAmiga` (`Core/VAmiga.h:1615`). You drive media through three of its member sub-APIs: `mem` (`MemoryAPI`), `df0..df3` (`FloppyDriveAPI`), and `amiga` (`AmigaAPI` for snapshots). All media-loading calls go through the `VAmiga` instance, never directly to internal core objects.

## THREADING (critical, read first)

Every media API method is annotated `VAMIGA_PUBLIC_SUSPEND` (`Core/VAmiga.cpp:45`), which expands to a sanity assert plus a `SuspendResume` RAII guard (`Core/VAmiga.cpp:36-43`):
- `assert(!emu || emu->isUserThread())` — these methods **must be called from outside the emulator thread** (i.e. from your bridge/UI thread, NOT from inside the emu pthread run-loop). In debug builds, calling from the emu thread asserts.
- The guard calls `emu->suspend()` on entry and `emu->resume()` on scope exit. Suspend only takes effect after the current frame completes, so a load can introduce a one-frame lag. You do NOT need to manually pause/suspend around these calls — the core does it.
- Each also calls `emu->markAsDirty()`.

So: ROM load, disk insert/eject, snapshot save/load are all called from your bridge thread (the thread that owns the `VAmiga` object), and are internally frame-synchronized. This matches how the IIGS bridge marshals onto its emu thread — here you do the opposite: call from outside.

Note the web `main.cpp` bypasses the API in the hot path and pokes `drive->swapDisk(...)` directly on the internal `FloppyDrive` (it runs single-threaded WASM). For the native multithreaded app, **use the `FloppyDriveAPI` methods**, not the raw `drive->` calls.

## (1) LOADING THE KICKSTART ROM

`MemoryAPI` (`Core/VAmiga.h:473-538`) exposes three overloads each for the main ROM and the extension ROM. Reach them via `vamiga.mem`:

```cpp
void loadRom(const fs::path &path);     // VAmiga.h:512  — from a file path
void loadRom(MediaFile &file);          // VAmiga.h:517  — from a RomFile wrapper
void loadRom(const u8 *buf, isize len); // VAmiga.h:522  — from a raw byte buffer
void loadExt(...);                      // same 3 overloads, VAmiga.h:513/518/523
```
ROM type (Kickstart vs boot vs ext) is auto-detected; you don't pass a type. Implementations: `Core/VAmiga.cpp:653-698`. Each is `VAMIGA_PUBLIC_SUSPEND` then `mem->loadRom(...)` + `markAsDirty()`.

For your 512KB Kickstart 3.1, the simplest two patterns:

**Buffer (recommended — bundle ROM in app, read into memory):**
```cpp
vamiga.mem.loadRom(romBytes, romLen);   // romLen == 512*1024
```

**Wrapper (gives you error detection before flashing) — the pattern main.cpp uses (`main.cpp:1753-1772`):**
```cpp
RomFile *rom = new RomFile(blob, len);   // throws AppError on bad data
if (vamiga.isPoweredOn()) { vamiga.powerOff(); vamiga.emu->update(); }
vamiga.mem.loadRom(*rom);                // flashes into Kickstart memory
// vamiga.mem.getRomTraits().title  -> e.g. "Kickstart 3.1"
delete rom;
vamiga.powerOn();
vamiga.run();
```
`RomFile` ctors (`Core/Media/RomFiles/RomFile.h:38-39`): `RomFile(const fs::path&)` and `RomFile(const u8 *buf, isize len)` — both `throws`.

ROM acceptance (`RomFile::isCompatible`, `RomFile.cpp:73-136`): valid sizes are 8KB/16KB (boot), 256KB/512KB (Kickstart), and 256KB+11 / 512KB+11 (Cloanto-encrypted). Header bytes are matched against `kickRomHeaders[11][7]` (`RomFile.cpp:33-61`); the Kickstart 3.1 signature is `{0x11,0x14,0x4E,0xF9,0x00,0xF8,0x00}` ("Kickstart 2.04, 3.1"). If the build flag `ALLOW_ALL_ROMS` is set, unknown 512KB blobs are also accepted.

Encrypted ROMs: `RomFile::isEncrypted()` / `decrypt()` (`RomFile.cpp:144-181`) need a sibling `rom.key` file; if you ship a plain (Cloanto-decrypted) 512KB Kickstart, ignore this. The `RomDatabase.h` (`Core/Media/RomFiles/RomDatabase.h`) maps CRC32 → human title used by `getRomTraits()`.

To check the machine is bootable after loading ROM: `vamiga.isReady()` (`VAmiga.h:1729`) — **throws** `AppError(Fault::ROM_MISSING)` / `CHIP_RAM_MISSING` if not runnable. main.cpp wraps it in `try{ isReady(); }catch(...){ wasRunnable=false; }` (`main.cpp:1751`). After a successful ROM load, if it was not runnable before and is ready now, it powers on (`main.cpp:1800-1803`).

Sanity check the IIGS-equivalent of "did the ROM take": `vamiga.mem.getRomTraits()` returns `RomTraits` (`VAmiga.h:501`); `.title` is the printable name. `deleteRom()`/`deleteExt()` (`VAmiga.h:533-535`) clear it.

## (2) FLOPPY: CREATE FROM .adf → INSERT INTO df0 → EJECT

Drives are `vamiga.df0..df3` (type `FloppyDriveAPI`, `Core/VAmiga.h:641-706`), also indexable via `vamiga.df[0..3]` (`VAmiga.h:1652`). Each wraps an internal `FloppyDrive*` at `.drive`.

### Insert — recommended host pattern (uses the API, frame-synced)

The cleanest path is `insertMedia`, feeding it an `ADFFile` (a `MediaFile`):
```cpp
ADFFile adf{blob, len};                  // ctor throws AppError on bad ADF
vamiga.df0.insertMedia(adf, /*wp=*/false);   // VAmiga.h:688, impl VAmiga.cpp:1171
```
`insertMedia` body (`VAmiga.cpp:1171-1176`): suspend → `drive->insertMediaFile(file, wp)` → `markAsDirty()`. The `wp` bool sets write-protection.

Alternative one-liner straight from a path (auto-detects type, decompresses ADZ/DMS, etc.):
```cpp
vamiga.df0.insert(adfPath, /*wp=*/false);    // VAmiga.h:689, impl VAmiga.cpp:1179
```
`insert` body: `drive->swapDisk(path)` then sets write-protection on the resulting disk.

`FloppyDriveAPI` insert/eject surface (`VAmiga.h:676-701`):
```cpp
void insertBlankDisk(FSFormat fstype, BootBlockId id, string name, const fs::path &path = {}); // :682
void insertMedia(MediaFile &file, bool wp);          // :688
void insert(const fs::path &path, bool wp);          // :689
void insertFileSystem(const MutableFileSystem &fs, bool wp); // :695
void ejectDisk();                                    // :699
MediaFile *exportDisk(FileType type);                // :701  (e.g. export back to .adf)
bool isInsertable(Diameter t, Density d) const;      // :674
FloppyDisk &getDisk() const;                         // :661  (nullptr-free? returns ref; guard with hasDisk)
```

### ADFFile constructors (`Core/Media/DiskFiles/ADFFile.h:47-60`)
```cpp
ADFFile(const fs::path &path) throws;                 // :48
ADFFile(const u8 *buf, isize len) throws;             // :49
ADFFile(Diameter dia, Density den) throws;            // :50  (blank)
ADFFile(const FloppyDisk &disk) throws;               // :52  (capture inserted disk → export)
ADFFile(const FloppyDrive &drive) throws;             // :53
ADFFile(const MutableFileSystem &volume) throws;      // :54
```
Standard DD ADF size = `ADFSIZE_35_DD = 901120` (880KB) (`ADFFile.h:22`). Battle Chess is a standard DD .adf → 901120 bytes → `Diameter::INCH_35`, `Density::DD`. HD = 1802240.

### Eject
```cpp
vamiga.df0.ejectDisk();                  // VAmiga.h:699, impl VAmiga.cpp:1188 (suspend + drive->ejectDisk())
```
Query presence first via info: `vamiga.df0.getInfo().hasDisk` (`bool`) — exactly what main.cpp checks before eject (`main.cpp:1026-1027`) and export (`main.cpp:1139`).

### df0 density constraint (important)
df0 only accepts **DD** disks by default (`FloppyDriveType::DD_35`). main.cpp (`main.cpp:1560-1583`) sets the drive type for HD disks and refuses HD-into-df0. For Battle Chess (DD) this is a non-issue. Drive type option:
```cpp
vamiga.set(Opt::DRIVE_TYPE, (i64)FloppyDriveType::DD_35, /*id=*/driveNr);
```
`FloppyDriveType` enum (`Core/Peripherals/Drive/FloppyDriveTypes.h:22-27`): `DD_35=0, HD_35=1, DD_525=2`.

### The raw-`drive->` web pattern (FYI, not for native multithreaded use)
main.cpp builds a `std::unique_ptr<FloppyDisk>` then `wrapper->emu->df0.drive->swapDisk(std::move(disk))` (`main.cpp:1569`). The disk is built in `load_disk()` (`main.cpp:1476-1529`) by sniffing the format then `std::make_unique<FloppyDisk>(adf)` etc. `FloppyDisk` ctors (`Core/Peripherals/Drive/FloppyDisk.h:96-100`): `FloppyDisk(const FloppyFile &file, bool wp=false)`. The internal `FloppyDrive::swapDisk` overloads (`FloppyDrive.h:379-381`) take `unique_ptr<FloppyDisk>`, `FloppyFile&`, or `fs::path`. Prefer the public `FloppyDriveAPI` for the native app.

```
UNKNOWN, WORKSPACE, SNAPSHOT, SCRIPT, ADF, ADZ, EADF, HDF, HDZ, IMG, ST, DMS, EXE, DIR, ROM
```
(`enum class FileType : long`). Human strings via `FileTypeEnum::help()` and `_key()` (`MediaFileTypes.h:44-87`): ADF="Amiga Disk File", HDF="Hard Disk File", DMS="Disk Masher System", ROM="Kickstart ROM".

- Factories (`MediaFile.h:35-40`, impl `MediaFile.cpp:37-128`):

  ```cpp
  static MediaFile *make(const fs::path &path);                  // auto-detect
  static MediaFile *make(const fs::path &path, FileType type);

  static MediaFile *make(MutableFileSystem &fs, FileType type);  // ADF only
  ```

## (4) ERROR REPORTING — exceptions, not return codes

Header `Core/Infrastructure/Error.h`:
- `struct AppException : std::exception` (`Error.h:16`) with fields `i64 data;` and `string description;`, plus `what()`.
- `struct AppError : AppException` (`Error.h:32`) — **the one media code throws.** Constructed as `AppError(Fault fault, ...)`; `fault()` returns the `Fault` enum (`Error.h:40`), `what()` returns the description string.

`Fault` enum (`Core/Infrastructure/ErrorTypes.h:16`, `enum class Fault : long`) — media-relevant values:
- ROM: `ROM_MISSING`(73), `AROS_NO_EXTROM`(74), `MISSING_ROM_KEY`(116), `INVALID_ROM_KEY`(117), `CHIP_RAM_MISSING`(68)
- Disk: `DISK_MISSING`, `DISK_INCOMPATIBLE`, `DISK_INVALID_DIAMETER`, `DISK_INVALID_DENSITY`, `DISK_INVALID_LAYOUT`, `DISK_WRONG_SECTOR_COUNT`, `DISK_INVALID_SECTOR_NUMBER` (78-83)
- File: `FILE_NOT_FOUND`, `FILE_TYPE_MISMATCH`, `FILE_TYPE_UNSUPPORTED`, `FILE_CANT_READ/WRITE/CREATE` (57-65)
- HDF: `HDR_UNSUPPORTED_*`, `HDR_CORRUPTED_*` (87-97)
- Snapshot: `SNAP_CORRUPTED`(106)

Get the printable name with `FaultEnum::key(Fault)`. main.cpp's idiom (`main.cpp:1759-1762`):
```cpp
catch(AppError &exception) {
    Fault ec = Fault(exception.data);          // exception.data is the i64 fault code
    printf("%s\n", FaultEnum::key(ec));
}
```
`SyntaxError` is a separate type (used only by RetroShell/Headless arg parsing, `Headless.cpp:24`), not media. Headless `main()` catches `SyntaxError`, then `AppError` ("Emulator Error: ..."), then `std::exception` (`Headless.cpp:24-50`).

### Constructors that throw (wrap in try/catch)
- `RomFile(buf,len)` / `RomFile(path)` — bad size/header → `AppError`.
- `ADFFile(buf,len)` etc. — bad ADF → `AppError(Fault::DISK_*)`.
- `vamiga.isReady()` — throws if not bootable (no ROM / no RAM).
- `vamiga.amiga.loadSnapshot(...)` — on corrupt snapshot, the API itself does a `Cmd::HARD_RESET` then **re-throws** (`VAmiga.cpp:2334-2349`), so the machine is left reset, not in a half-loaded state. Catch and surface to the user.

## (5) SAVE-STATE (Snapshots) — bonus, since same lens

Via `vamiga.amiga` (`AmigaAPI`, `VAmiga.h:38-123`):
```cpp
MediaFile *takeSnapshot();                 // VAmiga.h:75 — caller owns, must delete (VAmiga.cpp:2321)
void loadSnapshot(const MediaFile &snap);  // VAmiga.h:81  (VAmiga.cpp:2328)

```
main.cpp save path (`main.cpp:1263`): `snapshot = wrapper->emu->amiga.takeSnapshot();` then later `delete`. Load path (`main.cpp:1727-1731`): `Snapshot *file = new Snapshot(blob,len); vamiga.amiga.loadSnapshot(*file); delete file;`. `Snapshot::isCompatible(blob,len)` (`Media/Snapshot.h`) gates it, and main.cpp additionally excludes the case where the filename suffix is `rom` (`main.cpp:1722`). All `VAMIGA_PUBLIC_SUSPEND` → call from bridge thread.

## Concrete recommended boot sequence for the native bridge

```cpp
VAmiga vamiga;
vamiga.launch(self, messageCallback);              // starts emu thread (VAmiga.h:1887)
vamiga.set(ConfigScheme::...);                     // pick an Amiga model (VAmiga.h:1921)
vamiga.mem.loadRom(kickstartBytes, 512*1024);      // (1) ROM from buffer
ADFFile adf{adfBytes, adfLen};                     // wrap Battle Chess .adf
vamiga.df0.insertMedia(adf, false);                // (2) insert into df0
try { vamiga.isReady(); } catch (AppError &e) { /* surface FaultEnum::key(e.fault()) */ }
vamiga.powerOn();
vamiga.run();
// eject later: vamiga.df0.ejectDisk();
```
All of the above run on the thread that owns `vamiga` (your bridge thread) — **not** inside the emu run-loop — because of the `VAMIGA_PUBLIC` user-thread assert + internal suspend/resume.

# vAmiga iOS Integration Map — RUN LOOP, THREADING & AUDIO

## 0. The critical threading fact (decides everything)

**The WASM build runs the core SINGLE-THREADED on the JS main thread.** `std::thread thread;` exists in `Thread.h:37`, but the WASM build's `Thread::launch()` is stubbed: `Thread.h:86 isLaunched() { return true; /*thread.joinable();*/ }` and `isEmulatorThread()/isUserThread()` both `return true` (`Thread.h:101-102`). The web host does **not** rely on the core's internal emulator thread — it drives frame production manually from `requestAnimationFrame` by directly calling `emu->emu->computeFrame()`.

**For the native iOS app you have two valid paths:**
- **Path A (mirror the web host, recommended):** Do NOT use the core's internal thread. Spawn your own pthread/`DispatchQueue` in `EmulatorBridge`, and on it run a manual loop that calls `computeFrame()` N times per display tick (same logic as `wasm_draw_one_frame`). This is what the WASM glue does and it's the most faithful, best-understood path. Pacing is host-driven.
- **Path B (use the core's real thread):** The non-WASM core has a real `Thread::runLoop()` (`Thread.h:128`) with `sleep()`/`wakeUp()` VSYNC-pulse synchronization (`VAmiga::wakeUp()` doc at `VAmiga.h:1858-1866`). You'd call `launch()` to spawn it, then send `wakeUp()` on each `CADisplayLink` tick. This is the macOS-app model. More moving parts; the WASM glue does not exercise it, so you have less reference code.

**Recommendation: Path A.** Below is mapped against the web host since that's the working reference, with Path B notes where relevant.

---

## 1. The exact main loop

### Web host loop (the reference): `requestAnimationFrame` → `wasm_draw_one_frame(now)`
There is **no `emscripten_set_main_loop`** in this build (the old call is commented out, `main.cpp:1937`). Instead, JS drives it:

- `js/vAmiga_ui.js:1988` `do_animation_frame(now)` → calls `calculate_and_render(now)` → `requestAnimationFrame(do_animation_frame)` (self-rescheduling, `:1994`). So the host tick = **display refresh rate** (typ. 60Hz; ProMotion 120Hz on iOS — relevant below).
- `:1976` `let behind = Module._wasm_draw_one_frame(now);` then `render_frame(now)` and drains overflow via `setTimeout(execute_amiga_frame)` (`:1980-1984`).

### `wasm_draw_one_frame(double now)` — `main.cpp:402-579` — the heart of pacing

The cadence is **decoupled from the host refresh rate** and locked to the emulated machine via a wall-clock frame budget:

```c
// main.cpp:417-418
double elapsedTimeInSeconds = (now - start_time)/1000.0;
int64_t targetFrameCount = (int64_t)(elapsedTimeInSeconds * target_fps * speed_boost);
```
- `target_fps` = **PAL 50.0 / NTSC 60.0** (`main.cpp:64-67`; `PAL_FPS 50.0`, `NTSC_FPS 60.0`). Set per video format in `wasm_set_display()` (`main.cpp:1360,1377`) and on the `Msg::VIDEO_FORMAT` message (`main.cpp:710-717`).
- Each tick computes how many emulator frames *should* have been produced by now (`targetFrameCount`) vs how many were (`total_executed_frame_count`), and runs the difference:

```c
// main.cpp:536-549  (non-vsync path)
behind = targetFrameCount - total_executed_frame_count;
if (behind<=0 && executed_since_last_host_frame==0) return -1; // ahead → skip render
...
if (behind>0) { emu->emu->computeFrame(); executed_frame_count++; total_executed_frame_count++; behind--; }
```
`computeFrame()` = **emulate exactly one full Amiga video frame** (`Emulator::computeFrame()` declared `Emulator.h:125`, overrides `Thread::computeFrame()` `Thread.h:119`). Return value `behind` (>0) tells JS to run extra frames out-of-band via `setTimeout`.

`emu->emu->update()` (`main.cpp:420`) is called **once at the top of every tick** — it drains the command queue (`CmdQueue`) and applies config/power/input commands. **You must call `update()` every tick** (and after any `set()`/`put()`/`keyboard`/`disk` op — the glue does this religiously, e.g. `main.cpp:937,963,1331`).

### Resync / catch-up guard
- `MAX_GAP 5` (`main.cpp:389`): if `targetFrameCount - total_executed > 5`, it's hopelessly behind → reset `start_time=now`, `total_executed_frame_count=0`, `targetFrameCount=1` (`main.cpp:449-464`). Prevents spiral-of-death after a stall (app backgrounded, etc.).
- Warp: `if (emu->isWarping())` runs up to 25 `computeFrame()` per tick (`main.cpp:423-436`).

### Key cadence facts for iOS
- **Emulated frame rate is fixed: 50Hz PAL / 60Hz NTSC**, independent of your `CADisplayLink`.
- On a **120Hz ProMotion** display you'll get ~120 ticks/s but `targetFrameCount` math still only asks for ~50/60 `computeFrame()` calls/s — most ticks render-only. Set `CADisplayLink.preferredFramesPerSecond` to 60 (or leave adaptive) and reuse this exact wall-clock-budget algorithm. Do **not** call `computeFrame` once per display tick blindly or PAL will run 20% fast on 60Hz.
- Use `CACurrentMediaTime()*1000.0` (ms) as your `now`. The web `mach_absolute_time()` shim (`main.cpp:917-922`) confirms time is plain wall-clock ms internally.

### Path B cadence (if using the core thread)
`Thread::runLoop()` calls `sleep()` (blocks until wakeup) then `execute()` (runs `missingFrames()` frames). You'd call `vamiga.wakeUp()` (`VAmiga.h:1866`) on each VSYNC/`CADisplayLink` tick "right after the current texture has been handed to the GPU" (per the doc comment). `missingFrames()` (`Emulator.h:124`) does the same target-vs-actual math internally.

---

## 2. Frame → host display synchronization

### Texture acquisition
- Pixel pointer: `wasm_pixel_buffer()` → `emu->emu->getTexture().pixels.ptr` (`main.cpp:871-876`). Type `Texel*`.
- `Emulator::getTexture()` returns `const Texture&` (`Emulator.h:171`). `Texture` (`Texture.h:22-62`): `Buffer<Texel> pixels`, `i64 nr` (frame number), `bool lof`, `bool prevlof`.
- **Pixel format: `Texel = u32`** (TPP=1, `config.h:38`; `FrameBufferTypes.h:31`). Each texel is a 32-bit RGBA value, memory order `0xAABBGGRR` little-endian (web treats it as RGBA bytes; the calibration code reads `0xFF0000FF`=red as `0xAARRGGBB`-ish — verify channel order against your Metal `MTLPixelFormat`; web GL uses it directly as RGBA8). For iOS Metal, upload as `.bgra8Unorm` or `.rgba8Unorm` and confirm visually — the IIGS sibling app's format choice is your tiebreaker.

### Texture dimensions (full emulator texture)
From `Constants.h`:
- `HPIXELS = 912` (= 4 × HPOS_CNT 228), `VPIXELS = 313` (`Constants.h:89-90`). `PIXELS = VPIXELS*HPIXELS` (`:91`).
- So the **full framebuffer is 912 × 313 texels = 285,456 × 4 bytes ≈ 1.14 MB** (with TPP=1). Stride = `HPIXELS*TPP = 912` texels/row.
- The doc at `VAmiga.h:1126-1129` says use `vamiga::Texture::width`/`::height` constants — but in this build the texture is `HPIXELS × VPIXELS`. Confirm the exact `Buffer<Texel>` size at runtime via `getTexture().pixels.size`.

### Cropping / viewport (the visible sub-rect)
You display only a sub-rectangle. The web host computes `xOff, yOff, clipped_width, clipped_height` and crops in the renderer. For a first iOS pass, use a fixed geometry (mirror `DISPLAY_STANDARD`, `main.cpp:1413-1425`): `xOff=208+HBLANK_MAX`, `yOff=PAL::VBLANK_CNT+10`, etc. The adaptive "viewport tracking" path (`Msg::VIEWPORT`, `main.cpp:657-709`) is a later refinement — defer it.

### Tearing / frame-ready signal
- `wasm_frame_info()` (`main.cpp:877-893`) packs `texture.nr` (frame counter) + `lof`/`prevlof` bits. The worker path uses it to detect "is there a new frame to render?" (`vAmiga_ui.js:1963-1968`). On iOS: compare `getTexture().nr` to last-rendered nr; only re-upload to Metal when it changed.
- **Texture locking:** `VideoPortAPI::lockTexture()/unlockTexture()` (`VAmiga.h:1115-1122`) and `Emulator::lockTexture()` (`Emulator.h:172-173`, a `util::Mutex textureLock`). **In Path A (single emulation thread you own) you don't strictly need the lock** since you control when `computeFrame` runs vs when you upload. In Path B (core thread producing async) you MUST `lockTexture()` around the GPU upload. The web host (single-threaded) never locks — confirms Path A doesn't need it.

## 3. AUDIO — pull model, format, pump

### The host PULLS samples; the core never pushes audio.
The Amiga frame production (`computeFrame`) fills an internal stereo ring buffer; the host drains it on its own cadence.

### Core API: `AudioPortAPI` (`VAmiga.h:975-1038`), instance `emu->audioPort`
Three drain functions (all return `isize` = samples actually copied):
```cpp
isize copyMono(float *buffer, isize n);                 // VAmiga.h:1006
isize copyStereo(float *left, float *right, isize n);   // VAmiga.h:1014  ← web uses this
isize copyInterleaved(float *buffer, isize n);          // VAmiga.h:1025  ← best for iOS AudioUnit
```
- **Sample format: 32-bit float.** Stereo. `copyStereo` = planar (separate L/R buffers). `copyInterleaved` = LRLRLR… in one buffer.
- Underlying buffer: `AudioStream stream = AudioStream(4096)` (`AudioPort.h:130`) — a `ResizableRingBuffer<SamplePair>` where `SamplePair { float l; float r; }` (`AudioStream.h:26-32`). So natively interleaved float L/R pairs, capacity 4096 pairs (resizable).
- **Fill level / available count:** `emu->audioPort.port->stream.count()` (`main.cpp:1302`) returns # of stereo SamplePairs currently buffered. Use this to decide how many to pull.

### Sample rate — host tells the core its rate
- Core default `sampleRate = 44100.0` (`AudioPort.h:88`). **You must set it to the device rate**:
```c
// main.cpp:2211-2217
wasm_set_sample_rate(unsigned sr):
    emu->set(Opt::HOST_SAMPLE_RATE, sample_rate);   // ← the call you need
    emu->emu->update();
```
- Web passes `audioContext.sampleRate` (`vAmiga_ui.js:2095,2185`), typically 44100 or 48000. **On iOS pass `AVAudioSession.sharedInstance().sampleRate`** (typically 48000) into `Opt::HOST_SAMPLE_RATE`, then `update()`.
- The core has **Adaptive Sample Rate (ASR)** (`Opt::AUD_ASR`, `AudioPort.h:78,90-92`) that auto-corrects drift between emulation pace and host pace — leave it on (default) so you don't get buffer under/overflows.
- Master volume must be unmuted: `emu->set(Opt::AUD_VOLL,100); set(Opt::AUD_VOLR,100)` (`main.cpp:784-785`), and after resume the web explicitly calls `audioPort.port->unmute(10000)` (`main.cpp:2671`). `AudioPort::unmute(steps)` fades in (`AudioPort.h:293`).

### Web pump #1: AudioWorklet pull, 1024-frame chunks (`main.cpp:1300-1325`)
```c
unsigned wasm_copy_into_sound_buffer():
    auto count = emu->audioPort.port->stream.count();
    for (ipos=1024; ipos<=count; ipos+=1024)
        emu->audioPort.copyStereo(sound_buffer+copied, sound_buffer+copied+1024, 1024);
        copied += 1024*2;
    return copied/2;
```
- Drains in **1024-sample blocks**, planar into a flat `float sound_buffer[16384*2]` (`main.cpp:1294`). The worklet (`vAmiga_ui.js:2126-2163`) requests blocks, ships 2048-float Float32Array "shuttles" (1024 L + 1024 R) to the `AudioWorkletProcessor` (`js/vAmiga_audioprocessor.js`).
- JS sound-buffer slots: 16 slots × 2048 floats (`vAmiga_ui.js:2117-2121`).

### Web pump #2: SharedArrayBuffer variant (`main.cpp:2469-2493`)
```c
wasm_update_audio(int offset):
    emu->audioPort.copyStereo(leftChannel.ptr+offset, rightChannel.ptr+offset, leftChannel.size/2);
```
- Planar `Buffer<float> leftChannel/rightChannel`, lazily `init(2048,0)` (`main.cpp:2469-2483`).

### iOS audio recommendation
Drive the pull from a **real-time audio callback** (`AVAudioEngine` source node, or a `RemoteIO`/`AURenderCallback`), NOT from the render loop:
1. On audio session setup: `emu->set(Opt::HOST_SAMPLE_RATE, (i64)session.sampleRate); emu->emu->update();`
2. In the render callback for `frameCount` frames into an interleaved float `*outBuffer`: `isize got = emu->audioPort.copyInterleaved(outBuffer, frameCount);` then zero-fill `(frameCount-got)` on underrun.
3. Use `.float32` interleaved stereo `AVAudioFormat`. ASR keeps the ring buffer from starving/overflowing relative to your 50/60Hz `computeFrame` cadence.
This decouples audio (real-time thread, 48kHz) from video (your emulation thread, 50/60 fps) — same separation the web build achieves via the AudioWorklet thread.

## 4. Host callbacks — the MsgQueue listener

### Registration: `launch(listener, callback)`
```c
// main.cpp:860
wrapper->emu->launch(wrapper->emu, &theListener);
```
- Signature: `void VAmiga::launch(const void *listener, Callback *func)` (`VAmiga.h:1887`).
- **`Callback` typedef: `typedef void Callback(const void *, Message);`** (`MsgQueueTypes.h:289`).
- `launch()` (per doc `VAmiga.h:1873-1887`) launches the emulator thread (no-op thread in WASM) **and registers the message receiver**. Call it once at init, before `powerOn`.

### The listener: `theListener(const void *emu, Message msg)` (`main.cpp:654-746`)
`Message` struct (`MsgQueueTypes.h:266-282`):
```c
struct {
  Msg type;                                  // the enum tag
  union {
    struct { i64 value; i64 value2; };       // generic payload
    CpuMsg cpu;                              // { u32 pc; u8 vector; }
    DriveMsg drive;                         // { i16 nr; i16 value; i16 volume; i16 pan; }
    HdcMsg hdc;                             // { i16 nr; HdcState state; }
    ScriptMsg script;                       // { isize line; i16 delay; }
    SnapshotMsg snapshot;                   // { void *snapshot; }
    ViewportMsg viewport;                   // { i16 hstrt; i16 vstrt; i16 hstop; i16 vstop; }
  };
};
```
Read the tag via `msg.type`, decode the right union member. `MsgEnum::key(msg.type)` → string name (`main.cpp:730`, `MsgQueueTypes.h:_key`).

### Two ways to consume messages
1. **Push (callback):** the `theListener` you register fires on each message. **Threading caveat:** in the real core this fires on the emulator thread, so on iOS marshal to the main thread before touching UIKit/SwiftUI (web does the analogous post-to-UI-thread, `main.cpp:633`). Mirror the IIGS bridge's dispatch pattern.
2. **Pull (poll):** `MsgQueueAPI::getMsg(Message&)` (`VAmiga.h:1166`) / `AmigaAPI::getMsg` (`VAmiga.h:122`) — returns false when empty. With `lockMsgQueue()/unlockMsgQueue()` (`VAmiga.h:1158-1162`). You could drain this once per render tick on the main thread to avoid cross-thread UI dispatch entirely.

### Messages the host acts on (from `theListener` + Headless)
- `Msg::VIEWPORT` (`main.cpp:657`) → adaptive crop rect (`msg.viewport.hstrt/vstrt/hstop/vstop`).
- `Msg::VIDEO_FORMAT` (`main.cpp:710`) → PAL/NTSC switch; **drives `target_fps` change** — wire this to your loop's `target_fps`. `TV(msg.value)` == `TV::NTSC`/`TV::PAL`.
- `Msg::DRIVE_STEP / DRIVE_POLL / HDR_STEP` (`main.cpp:718`) → decode `msg.drive.nr`, `msg.drive.value`.
- `Msg::SER_OUT` (`main.cpp:732`) → serial output bytes.
- Power/run/LED/disk/etc. names (full list `MsgQueueTypes.h:200-243`): `DRIVE_LED`, `DRIVE_MOTOR`, `DISK_INSERT`, `DISK_EJECT`, `DISK_PROTECTED`, `HDC_STATE`, `SNAPSHOT_TAKEN`, `SNAPSHOT_RESTORED`, `RECORDING_*`, `SHAKING`, `ALARM`, `MON_SETTING`, plus power/config/run-state messages. The IIGS app maps the analogous set to drive-LED indicators, power button state, etc.
- Headless (`Headless.cpp:162-190`) shows the minimal pattern: switch on `msg.type`, handle `RSH_ERROR`/`ABORT`, default ignore. Use this as your skeleton.

### "Ready to run" handshake
On successful ROM load the web build synthesizes `MSG_READY_TO_RUN` to JS (`main.cpp:1806-1808`). Native equiv: after `mem.loadRom()` + `isReady()` succeeds, `powerOn()` then notify your UI.

## 5. State-control surface (for the bridge's lifecycle methods)

All on `VAmiga` (`VAmiga.h:1736-1804`), each followed by `emu->emu->update()` where the glue does:
- `powerOn()` / `powerOff()` (`:1742/1749`) — powerOn leaves state PAUSED.
- `run()` (`:1759`) — to RUNNING; implicit powerOn if off. Web: `wasm_run` (`main.cpp:1918-1925`).
- `pause()` (`:1767`). Web: `wasm_halt` → `pause()` (`main.cpp:1906-1916`).
- `hardReset()` (`:1774`) — web `wasm_reset` (`main.cpp:1900-1903`); `softReset()` (`:1780`).
- `halt()` (`:1788`) — shutdown only.
- `warpOn/Off` (`:1792/1796`), `trackOn/Off` (`:1800/1804`).
- State queries: `isPoweredOn/Off`, `isPaused`, `isRunning`, `isWarping`, `isHalted`, `isReady()` (throws if ROM missing) — `VAmiga.h:1694-1729`; backed by `ExecState{OFF,PAUSED,RUNNING,HALTED}` (`ThreadTypes.h:20-26`).
- `wakeUp()` (`:1866`) — only needed in Path B.

**Lifecycle order (from `main()` + `wrapper->run()`, `main.cpp:848-868,765-809`):**
1. `new VAmiga()`
2. `emu->launch(listener, &theListener)` (registers callback)
3. configure: `set(Opt::AUD_VOLL/R,100)`, `set(Opt::MEM_CHIP_RAM,512)`, `set(Opt::MEM_SLOW_RAM,512)`, `set(Opt::AGNUS_REVISION, OCS)`, `set(Opt::DRIVE_CONNECT,true,{1})`, etc. → `emu->emu->update()`
4. `mem.loadRom(...)` → `isReady()` → `powerOn()` → `run()`
5. start your render/emulation loop.
